communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Maine Freedom of Access Act, I hereby request the following records:

created, sent, or received between March 26, 2024, and March 26, 2026:
1. All records and communications, including emails and meeting minutes, regarding the pricing, negotiation, renewal, or discontinuation of the Town’s contract with Delta Ambulance.
2. All records regarding the feasibility or planning of a town-operated municipal ambulance service, regional EMS partnerships, or seeking alternative EMS vendors (including all communications and documents associated with information finding).
3. All records, correspondence, and documents related to Selectman Alicia Collins’ role or position on the Delta Ambulance Board of Directors, including meeting agendas, minutes, or internal Delta communications received or sent via town-issued email addresses or devices.
4. Any analysis of Delta Ambulance’s service quality, performance evaluations, or financial comparisons (excluding protected patient information).
5. All responsive communications conducted by Town Select Board members (John Whitcomb, Alisa Meggison, Lewis Corriveau, Christopher Ellis, and Alicia Collins) using personal email, personal text messages, or other private platforms that relate to town business or Delta Ambulance operations as defined by 1 M.R.S. § 402(3).

I also request that, if appropriate, fees be waived as we believe this request is in the public interest, as suggested but not stipulated by 1 M.R.S.A. § 408(6). The requested documents will be made available to the general public free of charge as part of the public information service at MuckRock.com, and is not made for commercial usage.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

MuckRock

To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Maine Freedom of Access Act, I hereby request the following records:

created, sent, or received between March 26, 2024, and March 26, 2026:
1. All emails and correspondence sent to or from Alicia.Collins@legislature.maine.gov containing the keywords "Delta," "Delta Ambulance," "Delta Dispatch," "Ambulance Contract," or "EMS funding."
2. Any and all records, including meeting minutes, agendas, handouts, or slide decks provided to Representative Collins in her capacity as a member of the Delta Ambulance Board of Directors that were received or stored on state-issued devices or accounts.
3. All records and communications regarding legislative efforts, grant applications, or budget requests initiated or supported by Representative Collins to secure state, county, or municipal funding, subsidies, or financial assistance for Delta Ambulance.
4. All communications between Representative Collins and any representatives, employees, or lobbyists for Delta Ambulance regarding ambulance service operations, regional EMS partnerships, or municipal contract negotiations.
5. Any responsive communications conducted via personal email or text message that pertain to the transaction of public business (specifically regarding EMS funding or Delta Ambulance operations) as defined by 1 M.R.S. § 402(3).

I also request that, if appropriate, fees be waived as we believe this request is in the public interest, as suggested but not stipulated by 1 M.R.S.A. § 408(6). The requested documents will be made available to the general public free of charge as part of the public information service at MuckRock.com, and is not made for commercial usage.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

MuckRock
